codeReview.summary: Clean implementation that removes exactly the 15 console.log calls from src/App.js and deletes the dead test-tracking.js file. All console.error calls are correctly preserved. No accidental deletions of functional code. The diff shows 52 lines removed across 2 files (16 lines from App.js, 36 from test-tracking.js), all purely debug instrumentation. Post-change verification (426 tests, lint, format) all passed.

issues.description: After removing the two console.log calls at lines 56 and 59 (pre-edit), an extra blank line was also removed at line 60 (between the removed console.log('🎬 App component mounting...') and console.log('🚀 App initialization starting...')). This is fine cosmetically but technically one more line was removed than the 15 console.log lines described. The extra blank line removal is a non-functional improvement.

The `const startTime = Date.now();` variable at line 60 is now only used to compute `elapsed` at line 107 for the `remainingTime = Math.max(0, 800 - elapsed)` calculation. The variable still serves a functional purpose (the minimum loading delay), so this is fine. Just noting for completeness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md explicitly documents the rule 'Do NOT remove console.error calls from utility files' but App.js itself also contains console.error calls (6 instances at lines 70, 81, 92, 103, 167, 204). The rule should arguably say 'Do NOT remove console.error calls from any source files' to be complete, since App.js also has them.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/App.js has 6 console.error calls that were correctly preserved. AGENTS.md line 'Do NOT remove console.error calls from utility files' could be broader.

VAL-CLEAN-001.notes: Independently verified: grep for console.log in src/App.js returns zero matches. All 15 emoji-prefixed debug logs removed. All 6 console.error calls preserved.

VAL-CLEAN-002.notes: Independently verified: ls test-tracking.js returns 'No such file or directory'. The 36-line browser console test script was correctly deleted.

VAL-CLEAN-003.notes: Worker ran npm test and confirmed 426 tests pass, 19 test suites, 0 failures. Lint shows 0 errors (4 pre-existing warnings in test files). Format check passes.

summary: Feature console-cleanup-dead-file passes scrutiny. The worker correctly removed all 15 console.log calls from src/App.js, preserved all console.error calls, and deleted the unused test-tracking.js file. All three validation assertions are satisfied. No blocking issues found. Two minor non-functional observations noted (extra blank line removal, startTime variable still functional). Worker followed the code-hygiene-worker skill procedure correctly.
